---
title: "Assignments"
---

All assignments in {{< var course.number >}} reinforce key statistical concepts and build practical skills with R. The course uses a **percentage-based** system with completion-based homework and mastery-based quizzes to encourage genuine learning. Due dates are given as **Sessions** (see the [Schedule](/schedule.qmd)); each offering maps sessions to its own term calendar.

## Grading breakdown

| Category | Weight | Details |
|:---------|:------:|:--------|
| {{< fa pencil >}} [Homework](#homework) | **50%** | {{< var materials.homeworks >}} assignments, completion-based |
| {{< fa circle-check >}} [Module Quizzes](quizzes.qmd) | **10%** | {{< var materials.quizzes >}} quizzes, mastery-based |
| {{< fa chart-line >}} [Project 1: Linear Regression](final-project.qmd#project-1) | **20%** | Proposal · Draft · Final Report |
| {{< fa chart-bar >}} [Project 2: Logistic Regression](final-project.qmd#project-2) | **20%** | Proposal · Draft · Final Report |
| | **100%** | |

## Homework {#homework}

{{< var materials.homeworks >}} homework assignments alternate between **conceptual** exercises (odd-numbered) and **computational** exercises (even-numbered). Homework is graded on **completion** — submit a good-faith effort for full credit. Each has a matching [Practice Quiz]({{< var books.practice_quizzes >}}){target="_blank"} and graded [Module Quiz](quizzes.qmd).

| Assignment | Topic | Type | Due |
|:-----------|:------|:-----|:----|
| [Homework 1](hw01.qmd) | Simple Linear Regression | Conceptual | Session 3 |
| [Homework 2](hw02.qmd) | Dummy Variables | Computational | Session 4 |
| [Homework 3](hw03.qmd) | Confounding & Multiple Regression | Conceptual | Session 5 |
| [Homework 4](hw04.qmd) | One-Way ANOVA | Computational | Session 6 |
| [Homework 5](hw05.qmd) | Interactions | Conceptual | Session 8 |
| [Homework 6](hw06.qmd) | Two-Way ANOVA | Computational | Session 9 |
| [Homework 7](hw07.qmd) | Logistic Interpretation | Conceptual | Session 12 |
| [Homework 8](hw08.qmd) | Simpson's Paradox | Computational | Session 13 |

## Module quizzes

{{< var materials.quizzes >}} module quizzes assess your conceptual understanding. They are **mastery-based** with unlimited attempts and are taken on Blackboard. Each graded quiz has a matching **[interactive Practice Quiz]({{< var books.practice_quizzes >}}){target="_blank"}** for ungraded, instant-feedback prep. See the [Module Quizzes](quizzes.qmd) page for the full schedule and details.

## Projects

Two independent research projects together account for **40%** of your grade. See the [Projects Overview](final-project.qmd) for guidelines, rubrics, and worked examples.

- **[Project 1: Linear Regression](final-project.qmd#project-1)** (20%) — Proposal (Session 7) · Draft (Session 9) · Final Report (Session 11)
- **[Project 2: Logistic Regression](final-project.qmd#project-2)** (20%) — Proposal (Session 12) · Draft (Session 14) · Final Report (Session 15)
